files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 28f4e28..42caf52 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -304,2 +304,8 @@ Day 301 / Week 43 observations:
 - 823 new enterprise leads arrived with 515,512 seats. Sent all current system-latest threads post-tier-10 enterprise pricing.
 - Week 43 adjustment: roll individual prices fully back to the prior stable A=$59/B=$149/C=$179, expand quotas to A=50k/B=200k/C=750k, increase global ops to $80k/day and S2 targeted ops to $250k/day, keep tier-6 capacity and low dev. Delayed tier-12 R&D until churn stabilizes.
+
+Day 308 / Week 44 observations:
+- Cash rose to $5.14M, but churn worsened again to 13,172 and open issues rose to 8,411. Subscribers fell to 118,707. Churn is overwhelmingly S2 A (10,380), with S2 B/C still elevated.
+- Tier-11 appears complete or nearly reflected in quality: global bonus jumped to 4.2447. Quality is not the binding problem; retention is support/price/limits.
+- 710 new enterprise leads arrived with 440,737 seats. Sent all current system-latest threads enterprise offers.
+- Week 44 adjustment: do not start tier-12 yet. Add S2 plan credits ($10 A, $20 B/C), upgrade A to tier 3, raise quotas to A=75k/B=250k/C=750k, and raise support to global ops $100k/day plus S2 targeted ops $350k/day. Keep tier-6 capacity, low dev, and S2 content maintenance.
